top_signals.title: LNG Shipping Market Softened Last Week
top_signals.summary: LNG The LNG market has had a softer feel to it this week. On the BLNG1 Australia–Japan route, rates have eased by $4,000/day week-on-week to settle at $71,000/day. The BLNG2 US Gulf–Continent route marginally moved downwards from $90,100/day last Friday to $89,000/day today. The BLNG3 US Gulf–Japan route declined from $99,200 last Friday to $95,900/day ...

top_signals.title: Energy-Efficiency Measures First says DNV Maritime CEO
top_signals.summary: In an age of regulatory shift, geopolitical uncertainty and commercial pressure it can be difficult to keep decarbonisation efforts on track. How can you plan for tomorrow when today’s realities are so demanding? With the theme of Nor-Shipping 2027 front of mind…

top_signals.title: Canadians Skeptical of Carney’s Push to Open Airports to Private Investment
top_signals.summary: A slim majority of Canadians oppose allowing private investment in airports, a new poll shows, as Prime Minister Mark Carney explores selling stakes in the publicly owned facilities to unlock funding for major infrastructure projects.

top_signals.title: China-built world’s largest methanol dual-fuel container ship sets sail
top_signals.summary: The world’s largest methanol dual-fuel container ship independently built by China embarked on its commercial maiden voyage from Qingdao Port in Shandong province on Friday. top_signals.title: Singapore’s fuel oil stocks declined by 14% in June
top_signals.summary: Residual fuel oil stocks in Singapore averaged 14% lower in June than in May, data from Enterprise Singapore shows. top_signals.title: Gibraltar worried about RED impact on West Med bunker markets
top_signals.summary: Gibraltar Port Authority is concerned about the potential impact of the EU’s Renewable Energy Directive III on West Mediterranean bunker trades, which could prompt ship operators to refuel at non-European locations while achieving little decarbonization, according to its top official. Brussels’ flagship clean energy legislation has mandated EU-based companies to raise renewable energy supplies to ...

top_signals.title: Biofuel Bunker Snapshot: B30 prices sharply up on the week
top_signals.summary: Europe Rotterdam’s ZRE A-rebated B30-VLSFO (POMEME) price has increased by $27/mt in the past week. The Dutch port’s conventional VLSFO price has remained mostly steady. Meanwhile, Prima Markets has assessed a $21/mt drop in ARA’s POMEME barge price. The price of ZRE A tickets, which can be generated by supplying POMEME, has dropped from €110/mtCO2eq ...

alerts.title: Port of Tarragona achieves its first PERS Certification
alerts.summary: ESPO congratulates the Port of Tarragona (Spain) on achieving its first PERS certification under the EcoPorts environmental management standard. Having been an EcoPorts member since 2014, this marks an important milestone in the port’s environmental management journey. Isabelle Ryckbost, ESPO Secretary General, stated: “I would like to congratulate the Port of Tarragona for its PERS ...
